title: dalea capitata var. capitata
intro: Dalea capitata var. capitata, also known as the white prairie clover, is a perennial legume native to the prairies of central North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, as it has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve immune function, making it a potential natural remedy for cardiovascular disease and other conditions. Dalea capitata var. capitata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional medicine to make salves and ointments for topical application.
